“I felt a strong conviction that we had to be one team to make sure we are making progress here,” Pichai says.
Will it hurt users’ trust?” says Liz Reid, Google’s VP of search.
Gemini 2.5, released in March 2025, and Gemini 3, in November, sailed past its chatbot competitors in key benchmarks.
Portilla uses Nano Banana to generate images, and Gemini to build stories and voice the characters.
(Google has 190,000 employees; DeepMind has around 6,000.)"},{"url":"https://blog.google/company-news/inside-google/message-ceo/sundar-pichai-ai-impact-summit-2026/","title":"Sundar Pichai at the AI Impact Summit 2026","content":"Today CEO Sundar Pichai gave remarks at the opening ceremony of the AI Impact Summit.
AI can solve big problems like drug discovery, as shown by Google DeepMind's AlphaFold project.
That question led to AlphaFold.
This breakthrough didn't just win a Nobel Prize; it compressed decades of research into a database that is now open to the world.
We’ve trained 100 million people in digital skills, and our new Google AI Professional Certificate will help people master AI in their jobs, available globally.
AI Impact Summit 2026: How we’re partne…"},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/time_sundar-pichai-spoke-with-time-about-how-artificial-activity-7458576365605486594-Mbfm","title":"Sundar Pichai on AI decision-making and Google's role | TIME posted on the topic | LinkedIn","content":"You can update your choices at any time in your settings.
Sundar Pichai spoke with TIME about how artificial intelligence is reshaping decision-making, the rise of AI assistants, and Google’s role in building the technology responsibly.
Sundar Pichai, CEO of Google and Alphabet, took the stage at #GoogleIO and shared how we’re helping you get more done with Gemini.
Alphabet’s record-breaking $85B raise for Google’s AI business is a helluva good signal Alphabet just pulled off a colossal $85 billion stock sale tied to its AI efforts—one of the biggest moves we’ve seen in this space.
The move comes after Google indicated it could spend as much as $180–190 billion on capital expenditures this year, while Big Tech companies collectively are projected to invest roughly $700 billion into AI infrastructure."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8drzv37z4jo","title":"Don't blindly trust what AI tells you, Google boss tells BBC","content":"People should not "blindly trust" everything AI tools tell them, the boss of Google's parent company Alphabet has told the BBC.
In his interview with the BBC, Mr Pichai said there was some tension between how fast technology was being developed and how mitigations are built in to prevent potential harmful effects.
For Alphabet, Mr Pichai said managing that tension means being "bold and responsible at the same time".
"For example, we are open-sourcing technology which will allow you to detect whether an image is generated by AI," he said."},{"url":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/you-cant-rest-google-ceo-sundar-pichai-tells-employees-in-all-hands-meeting-says-2026-will-be-intense/articleshow/125502093.cms","title":"You can’t …: Google CEO Sundar Pichai tells employees in all-hands meeting - The Times of India","content":"Google CEO Sundar Pichai has cautioned employees about an "intense" 2026, citing fierce AI competition and cloud demand.
During the gathering, Pichai also addressed growing concerns about a potential AI investment bubble, a topic that is being increasingly debated among Silicon Valley executives and Wall Street analysts as skepticism mounts over whether trillions of dollars in projected AI spending can be justified.“Amid significant AI investments and market talk of a potential AI bubble burst, how are we thinking about ensuring long-term sustainability and profitability if the AI market doesn't mature as expected?” Pichai read aloud a question posed by an employee."}]}
